rationale: The signal set is dominated by Google autocomplete suggestions (95 entries) tracking generic search interest in morphs, care, breeding basics, and marketplace searches. YouTube data shows established coverage of morph identification, breeding setup, and genetic guides (multiple "ULTIMATE" and "COMPLETE" guides already published 1–8 years ago). One recent outlier: a video titled "Don't buy super cappuccino crested gecko it's a scam and waste of money" (1 year ago, 2,059 views) and "Why So Much Hate for Cappuccino Geckos? The Truth No One Wants to Say…" (4 months ago, 421 views) suggest simmering debate, but cappuccino genetics are already published (cappuccino-crested-gecko-genetics slug). No forum threads, no social-media heat, no trending queries, and no breeder blog posts with fresh angles. The autocomplete data reflects broad search volume but no actionable debate or knowledge gap; it merely confirms that people search for "crested gecko morphs," "breeding weight," and "cappuccino price"—all serviceable by existing content. Zero evidence of a specific misconception, contested breeding outcome, or identification challenge that hasn't been covered. I'm rejecting all three slots.
